free_text: Differential cross section measurements for hadron
therapy: 50 MeV/nucleon 12C reactions on H, C, O, Al,
thin Si  (~150 um thick, 2.2 cm diam.),
thick Si (~500 um thick, 2.2 cm diam.),
CsI      (75 mm long, conical 25 to 28 mm diam.).
The CsI was separated from the thick Si detector by a
The telescopes were positioned as follows:
1. one fixed at an angle of 3 degrees, 81.36 cm
behind the target, covering a solid angle of 0.45 msr
2. four positioned on a rotating mount, 28.75 cm
behind the target, covering angles from 5 to 39
degrees, each telescope had a solid angle of 3.67 msr
Beam monitor to detect X-rays from 7 um thick
The dE-E method was used in combination
with pulse shape analysis to identify the fragments.
Because of the design of the telescope, it was
possible to create dE-E maps using either the thin
and thick Si detectors, or the thick Si detector with
The cross sections on oxygen were reconstructed from
the measurements with Al and Al2O3 targets.
The total error is calculated as the
linear sum of the statistical and systematic errors,
maximizing correlations. The systematic error of the
data analysis was estimated by a GEANT4 simulation,
implementing the experimental setup. An addition
systematic error was introduced to account for the
pollution of the dE-E map by C-12 trails, unremovable
The differential cross section on PMMA was derived from
the measured cross section on carbon, hydrogen and
oxygen. The reconstructed cross section then was
compared with an experiment using a 500 um thick PMMA
